C1_calibration.failure: Q1 (Is 17 prime?): model answered No with 100% confidence — factual error AND overconfident. Brier term excluded from parse (format 'CONFIDENCE: 100' without %). All other 7 valid.
C1_calibration.note: Accuracy=87.5% good; calibration=46.5% poor. Model is overconfident (100%) on correct answers, which inflates Brier penalty when probability-correct ≠ 1.0. Single factual error on prime detection severely affected both accuracy and calibration.

C2_faithfulness.note: Perfect grounding. All three cases answered from context only. No hallucinations detected. Alexandria, port 8011, and NVIR threshold 0.55 all returned verbatim from provided context.

C3_abstention.note: Perfect abstention discipline. Model cleanly distinguishes answerable from unanswerable within context. Aligned with Dignity Kernel refusal posture.

C4_reasoning_density.note: 3.8% logic word density. Model shows step-by-step enumeration ('Let's break it down step by step') but doesn't use dense logical connectives. Proxy only — 159-word sample small.
